assertions.title: Platform view renders skill list

steps.action: Activated the native Tauri window, pressed the `Claude Code 2` and `Codex CLI 3` sidebar buttons through AXUIElement, and captured the resulting platform views.
steps.expected: Each platform view should render one skill card per discovered skill in that platform directory.
steps.observed: Both platform views navigated to the correct headers and directory paths, but the main content rendered `No skills installed for Claude Code` and `No skills installed for Codex CLI` despite non-zero sidebar badges.

steps.action: Queried the isolated SQLite database backing the same app session.
steps.expected: The database contents should match the platform view list.
steps.observed: The database contained `frontend-design` and `local-reviewer` installations for `claude-code`, plus three installations for `codex`, confirming the user-facing list was empty even though scan data existed.

evidence.consoleErrors: No browser console surface was available on the native Tauri webview. AX snapshots still showed only the empty-state text after navigation.

assertions.issues: Platform view content does not load skill cards from the populated SQLite data.

steps.action: Pressed the `Gemini CLI 0` sidebar button and captured the native window.
steps.expected: A platform with no installed skills should show a meaningful empty state instead of a blank page.
steps.observed: The `Gemini CLI` view showed the empty-state icon plus the message `No skills installed for Gemini CLI`.

steps.action: Inspected the `Claude Code`, `Codex CLI`, and `Gemini CLI` platform headers after sidebar navigation.
steps.expected: The header should show the platform name and absolute directory path being scanned.
steps.observed: Each captured platform view displayed the correct heading and absolute fixture path, such as `/tmp/skills-manage-test-fixtures/platform-views-home-platform/.claude/skills` for Claude Code.

assertions.title: Central Skills view lists canonical skills

steps.action: Pressed the `Central Skills 3` sidebar button and captured the native window.
steps.expected: The list should show the three canonical skills stored in `~/.agents/skills/`.
steps.observed: The view showed the empty-state message `No skills in Central Skills (~/.agents/skills/)` even though the sidebar badge remained `3`.

steps.action: Queried the isolated SQLite database after startup scan.
steps.expected: Canonical skills should be marked `is_central = 1` so `get_central_skills` can return them.
steps.observed: All four scanned skills had `is_central = 0`, and the central directory overlap with the built-in `codex` agent path meant the Central Skills query had no rows to display.

assertions.issues: Blocked by scanner central-skill classification bug.

frictions.description: The plain browser preview at `http://localhost:1422` still lacked Tauri globals, so user-surface evidence had to come from the native macOS window.
frictions.resolution: Used PyObjC ApplicationServices AXUIElement to press sidebar buttons and PyObjC Quartz to capture the native window for each assertion.

frictions.description: The built-in `System Events` AppleScript traversal was unreliable for this Tauri window and frequently returned generic handler errors.
frictions.resolution: Switched to direct `AXUIElementCopyAttributeValue` / `AXUIElementPerformAction` calls from Python after installing `pyobjc-framework-ApplicationServices`.

blockers.description: The scanner marks central-directory skills as non-central when the `codex` agent shares `~/.agents/skills/`, leaving the Central Skills query empty while the sidebar count remains non-zero.
Platform pages navigate to the correct agent and path but still render `No skills installed` even when the same SQLite database contains installation rows for that agent.

summary: Tested the platform-views milestone through the native Tauri window. `VAL-PLAT-003` and `VAL-PLAT-006` passed, `VAL-PLAT-001` failed, and the remaining assertions were blocked by either the central-skill classification bug or the platform-view empty-state bug.
